mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound (0.18%), the persistent monetary accommodation lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and channels liquidity into risk assets, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as the rate remains near zero.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or SPX posts negative mean 20d return while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for 6+ consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 9.0% while the pace of monthly PAYEMS job losses decelerates relative to its trailing 3-month average, markets interpret this as capitulation in the labor market bottoming process, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as forward-looking equities price a recovery ahead of the labor data.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 9.0% and 1-month PAYEMS decline is smaller in magnitude than trailing 3-month average decline
failure_condition: In triggered instances, mean 20d forward SPX return is not significantly greater than unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05), or triggered instances show negative mean return in more than 40% of walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: A joint inflection where INDPRO month-over-month decline shrinks concurrently with PAYEMS job losses decelerating signals a synchronized turning point in the industrial and labor cycle, which forward-predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because equity markets front-run confirmed recoveries in production and employment jointly rather than either series alone.
failure_condition: Joint deceleration signal (both series improving m/m) fails to produce positive mean 60d forward return in at least 55% of historical trough episodes, or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over either single-series model (delta IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock housing recovery regime, once HOUST's 3-month moving average stops declining after a multi-year collapse (from prior peaks near 2000 thousand units to the current 458 thousand), this bottoming signals stabilization in residential investment that leads broader demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of HOUST stops declining for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: 60d forward SPX returns following HOUST stabilization signal are not significantly positive (p > 0.05) across historical post-recession episodes, or the signal reverses (HOUST resumes decline) in more than 50% of triggered cases within 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of positive month-over-month growth in RSAFS after a severe retail contraction indicates a rebound in consumer demand that precedes broader economic recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as consumption strength feeds into corporate earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth positive for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS 2-month positive turn signal and forward 20d SPX return is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean forward return conditional on trigger is not distinguishable from unconditional mean at 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable CPIAUCSL reading (disinflationary environment near 212.7) removes inflation-driven pressure for the Fed to reverse near-zero rates, reassuring equity markets that accommodative policy will persist, producing modest positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 5d forward SPX return during periods of low/stable CPI (m/m change within +/-0.2%) is not positive and statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is held below 0.25% simultaneously with UNRATE above 9%, the combination signals that policy accommodation will persist for an extended period due to labor market slack, which anchors low discount rates and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days beyond what either variable predicts alone.
regime_hint: zero-rate policy with high unemployment slack
failure_condition: Interaction model's Rank IC does not exceed the best single-series (FEDFUNDS or UNRATE) model by at least 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows, or forward returns conditional on joint trigger are negative in more than 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter contraction is smaller in magnitude than the prior quarter's contraction, it signals the recession is decelerating and approaching a trough, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as markets price in an approaching recovery regime ahead of confirmed GDP growth.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter decline is smaller in absolute magnitude than the prior quarter's decline
failure_condition: 60d forward SPX returns following a deceleration-in-contraction GDPC1 print are not significantly positive (p > 0.05) across historical recession-trough episodes, or the signal precedes a further GDP growth deterioration in more than 50% of cases.
